title: Classified Balance Sheet: Current Ratio
event: Reyes Co.'s classified balance sheet shows current assets of $90,000 and current liabilities of $60,000. Compute the current ratio.

computationPaths.narration: The current ratio tests short-term debt-paying ability: current assets available per dollar of current liabilities due.

memorize.body: Current ratio = Current assets ÷ Current liabilities
Current = converts to cash or comes due within one year (or the operating cycle, if longer).

questions.prompt: Current assets $90,000, current liabilities $60,000. Current ratio = ?

distractors.feedback: You inverted it — liabilities over assets. Assets go on TOP: 90 ÷ 60 = 1.5.
That's working capital (CA − CL), a dollar amount. The current RATIO divides: 1.5.
